Land Rover Discovery Sport D180 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery Sport D180 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned D180 (204DTD) engine typically costs £2,500–£4,500 supply only. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on workshop location. Main dealer new engines can exceed £7,000 supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery Sport D180 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is one of the more controversial engines in Land Rover's modern lineup. The 204DTD Ingenium diesel has a well-documented reputation for timing chain failure, turbocharger failure, and injector issues. Specialist rebuilders report seeing these engines regularly with catastrophic failures. However, with meticulous maintenance (oil changes every 8,000 miles) and preventative timing chain replacement, some examples can be reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Discovery Sport D180?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch and failure (the most common and costly issue), turbocharger failure (which can cause hydro-lock and bent conrods), fuel injector over-fuelling (which can melt pistons), DPF clogging (particularly with urban driving), and AdBlue system issues. The engine is highly sensitive to oil change intervals.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Sport D180 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold: if your Discovery Sport is worth £12,000 and a recon engine is £3,500 (29%), it's a clear keeper. If the vehicle is a tired £6,000 example, the same engine exceeds 50% — get a scrap valuation first. These are complex, valuable vehicles that often justify replacement spend.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery Sport D180 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The post-2019 D180 uses the 204DTD — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium diesel with mild-hybrid technology, producing 180 bhp (132 kW). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 204DTD timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,200) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap requiring a full engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Many specialists recommend replacing the chain as preventative maintenance at 80,000 miles.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery Sport D180 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only engine generation this badge ever used — the 204DTD MHEV. See the Era Map above for era-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery Sport D180 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Installation costs typically add £800–£1,500.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport D180 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Failure (204DTD)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or metallic noise from the engine on cold start, engine warning light, rough idling, loss of power.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — can fail at just 62,000 miles.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: at first sign of rattle, book inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The timing chain is a weak spot on Ingenium diesel engines. The components are prone to failure because of the weak materials used, and plastic particles from worn guides can block the oil pickup, causing spun bearings. Regular oil changes (every 8,000 miles, not the suggested 21,000–24,000) are critical. If you hear any rattle on startup, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: Turbocharger Failure
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, high-pitched whining noise, excessive smoke under load, sluggish acceleration.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; turbo failure can cause catastrophic engine damage.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The turbocharger on diesel Ingenium engines is composed of components "seemingly not sufficient for its purpose". A failed turbocharger can cause engine failure due to the intake of oil and debris, and excessive oil passing through the intake can hydro-lock the engine, bending conrods. If you notice any whistling, smoke, or power loss, have the turbo inspected immediately.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Over-fuelling
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, black smoke, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, loss of power.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The fuel injectors fitted to the 2.0 Ingenium engines have a tendency to over-fuel. This produces an extreme amount of heat as the large amount of fuel is compressed and ignited. The piston itself is comprised of a weaker than expected material and is very susceptible to damage — over-fuelling can cause the piston to melt. Regular servicing and using quality fuel can help mitigate this risk.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery Sport D180 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Replace turbo before it causes engine failure
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Bent conrods and damaged pistons require full rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This D180? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes every 8,000 miles or less
* Timing chain inspected and confirmed healthy
* No history of engine warning lights or AdBlue/DPF issues
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle or ticking — indicates timing chain issues
* Evidence of turbo failure (whistling, smoke, power loss)
* Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis, regardless of mileage

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 204DTD is a complex, high-performance engine that demands meticulous maintenance. With its well-documented timing chain, turbocharger, and injector vulnerabilities, buying one without a documented service history is a significant risk. A £3,500 reconditioned engine on a Discovery Sport valued at £12,000 is 29% of the car's value — worth it. On a sub-£6,000 example, think carefully. The 60% Land Rover scrap threshold applies. Some owners report the D180 as "really reliable" after initial software issues, but the engineering concerns remain.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport D180 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery Sport D180 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or metallic noise on startup (timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including oil changes every 8,000 miles (not the suggested 21,000–24,000)
* Turbo Check — Test acceleration; any whistling, smoke, or power loss?
* Smoke Check — Black smoke? (injector over-fuelling) / Blue smoke? (turbo oil leak)
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate DPF clogging
* AdBlue System — Check for warnings; post-2016 diesels can have AdBlue issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
